# Copilot code review — devantler-tech/skills

This repo is a curated, **agent-neutral index** of generic [agent skills](https://agentskills.io)
installable via `gh skill`, plus the publisher of devantler-tech's own in-house skills (e.g.
`ways-of-working`). Canonical conventions live in [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md); this is the concise
review checklist — flag violations of the rules below.

## Always (every PR)

- **Conventional-Commit PR title** (`feat:`/`fix:`/`chore:`/`ci:`/`docs:`/`refactor:`). On push to
  `main`, `release.yaml` derives the next version tag from the commit types, so a non-conventional or
  bracket-prefixed title is a defect. A `docs:`/`refactor:`-only change yields no version bump — that
  green "no release" skip is expected, not a failure.
- **Root-cause fixes only.** Flag anything that masks a failure: CI checks skipped or set
  `continue-on-error` to dodge red, weakened or removed validation, `--no-verify`, silenced linters.
- **No secrets in plaintext** — flag committed tokens, keys, or kubeconfigs.
- **Behaviour / command / structure change ⇒ its docs updated in the same PR.** A missing doc update
  is a review finding.
- PRs from the Daily AI Assistant carry a `> 🤖 Generated by the Daily AI Assistant` line — expected,
  not a finding.

## The README index is the source of truth

- The README `## Skills` tables are the **single source of truth** for what this repo offers.
  `scripts/install.sh` and the `setup-agent-skills` / `update-agent-skills` actions parse them directly.
- Any change to the catalogue updates the README tables — **never hand-maintain a parallel list**. Flag
  a skill row added or removed without the matching README change, or an `install.sh`↔README drift.
- **Prefer pointing at a canonical upstream** over re-hosting a copy; only add a root directory here for
  genuinely **in-house** skills. `gh skill` records the true source in `SKILL.md` frontmatter
  (`metadata.github-repo`/`github-path`/`github-ref`) — no lockfile, no sync bot, no custom metadata.

## Skills (agentskills.io spec)

- A skill directory lives at the **repo root** with a conformant `SKILL.md` (name, description, license
  frontmatter + body). CI validates against the agentskills.io spec (`skills-ref validate`) and
  `gh skill publish --dry-run` — flag a malformed or non-conformant `SKILL.md`.
- **Agent-neutral prose.** Keep in-house skills tool-neutral — flag Copilot/Claude/Cursor-only
  assumptions in the skill text; the same skill must work across every `gh skill` agent.

## GitHub Actions / CI

- **Pin every external action to a commit SHA** (`uses: owner/repo@<sha> # <version>`) — never floating
  tags; flag an unpinned or tag-only action.
- **`permissions: {}` at workflow top level**, granted per-job; `persist-credentials: false` on
  `actions/checkout` unless a job must push. Keep workflows `actionlint`-clean.
- `scripts/install.sh` must stay `shellcheck`-clean and `bash -n`-valid, and `./scripts/install.sh
  --list` must print a non-empty list (it parses the README index).

When unsure, defer to [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md).
